data.title: NLS Pharmaceutics announces pricing of private placement of up to $1M
data.description:
• NLS Pharmaceutics (NASDAQ:NLSP) announced the pricing of a private placement offering consisting of the issuance and sale of up to 322,580 common shares at a purchase price of $3.10 per common share, for gross proceeds of up to $1M.
  • The initial closing of the offering in the amount of $500,000 is expected to occur on or before January 10, 2025.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes.
  • NLSP shares were down 3.2% on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CHARBONE Hydrogen secures initial tranche of US$6M in convertible notes
data.description:
• CHARBONE Hydrogen (TSXV:CH:CA) announced on Wednesday that it has closed a $2.1M CAD unsecured convertible debt tranche to accelerate the funding of the construction of its first two green hydrogen production facilities.
  • The Sorel-Tracy facility will serve as CHARBONE's flagship green hydrogen production site, with operations scheduled to begin in the coming weeks, following delivery of a pre-ordered electrolyzer in Q1 2025.
  • The first tranche of the financing includes US$1.5 million (CA$2.1M) in gross proceeds through unsecured convertible notes with a 36-month term at a 12% annual interest rate, led by its US banker, maturing in December 2027.
  • The note offering closing of US$1.5 million(CA$2.1M)is part of a private placement led by its US banker of an aggregate principal amount up to US$6 million unsecured convertible notes for 36 months which shall bear interest at 12% per annum.

data.title: Zeta Global shares jump on huge boost in marketing platform engagement
data.description:
• Zeta Global (NYSE:ZETA) shares rose more than 9% on Wednesday after the marketing software company said it saw "record-setting engagement" to kick off the holiday shopping period.
  • From Thanksgiving to Cyber Monday, Zeta said its marketing platform saw a 108% year-over-year rise in usage, as companies turned to Zeta to aid in their marketing across various channels.
  • “With the holiday season being five days shorter, marketers are under increasing pressure to execute strategies that drive both value and high-impact results,” said Zeta Global Chief Executive and co-founder David Steinberg in a statement. “The Zeta Marketing Platform continues to empower brands to engage high-intent shoppers where they want to be reached, delivering exceptional efficiency and more effective outcomes during the most critical shopping days of the year.”

data.title: U.S. economic activity rises slightly since late October: Fed's Beige Book
data.description:
• Economic activity in the U.S. rose slightly across most of the Federal Reserve's 12 districts since late October, the central bank said in its latest Beige Book report.
  • That compared with the little changed economic activity across all districts in the previous Beige Book.
  • Three regions exhibited modest or moderate growth that offset flat or slightly declining activity in two others, the report noted.

data.title: Robinhood CEO Tenev 'keenly looking' into sports betting
data.description:
• Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD) CEO Vlad Tenev on Wednesday said his company is "keenly looking" into the sports betting space.
  • At the company's investor day event, Tenev said customers are interested in sports event contracts betting.
  • Sports betting in the U.S. generated revenue north of $11B in 2023. Up until 2018, Nevada was the only state providing the service, but now it is legal in 38 states and the District of Columbia, with active legislation surrounding sports betting in six more states.

data.title: Fed Chair Powell confident on having a good relationship with new Treasury secretary
data.description:
"I am confident that I'll have the same kind of relationship with him (Scott Bessent), once he's confirmed, that I've had with other Treasury secretaries," Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said Wednesday during a New York Times event.

The Fed Reserve Chair and Treasury secretary traditionally have lunch periodically. A good relationship is important because Fed officials and elected officials need to work together when dealing with crises, he said.

He declined to comment on President-elect Trump's previous comments about the Federal Reserve.

Updated at 2:00 PM ET: Powell emphasized that the central bank needs to be open in explaining officials' thinking on policy.

"We should be telling people what we're doing and why," he said.

Breaking news... check back for updates.

data.title: Hershey paring loss amid takeover speculation
data.description:
Shares of chocolate maker Hersey (NYSE:HSY) pared some of a loss on Wednesday amid some takeover speculation. Hershey fell 1.2%.

Hershey may have attracted some takeover interest, according to traders, who cited a Betaville "uncooked" alert that was circulating on Wednesday. The identity of the potential suitor wasn't known.

Hersey (HSY) is not new to takeover interest, though its last approach was in 2016, when Mondelez (MDLZ) tried to acquire it for $23 billion in cash and stock. The deal was ultimately scuttled as the Hershey Trust, which controls the company through voting rights, was against the deal.

Developing story ...

data.title: Foot Locker CEO remains confident in Nike partnership
data.description:
As a key supplier, Nike (NKE) shares were losing ground in early Wednesday trading in sympathy with disappointing Q3 results from Foot Locker (NYSE:FL). But shares for both have since pared their earlier losses as Foot Locker’s (FL) CEO Mary Dillon offered assurances that the Foot Locker (FL)/Nike (NKE) partnership is still a profitable one.

“We saw consumers remain cautious with their discretionary dollars,” CEO Mary Dillon said on the company’s earnings call with analysts, adding that spending was concentrated around the peak back-to-school season and then dried up afterward as shoppers withheld spending before the holiday season.

But while Nike (NKE) is still intricately entwined with Foot Locker’s (FL) success, Dillon expects the company to return to growth “on an allocation basis with Nike in Q4.”

Dillon conceded that there were short-term negative impacts on Foot Locker (FL) from Nike (NKE) and there still remains a high level of promotional pricing which is having a “cascading impact” on Foot Locker (FL). But Foot Locker (FL) is controlling Nike (NKE) inventory, optimizing receipt flow, and prioritizing full-price selling.

“On a strategic level, we are confident in the strength of our partnership with Nike,” Dillon assured analysts, and that despite recent softness as the company rebalances its product mix and inventory levels, Foot Locker (FL) remains confident in Nike’s (NKE) actions to benefit the brand as well as on the overall industry and partnership.

The company’s CFO agrees. “We are just really confident in the new offense that’s being put in place [with new Nike management],” Frank Bracken said, “and as we work into 2025, we feel that there will be a better sort of supply-and-demand balance,”

After trading with a loss of as much as 2.4%, Nike (NKE) shares are currently lower by 1.3%. Foot Locker (FL) has also cut its early losses by about half, last trading with a loss of 9%.

data.title: Novartis loses appeal to block generic Entresto launch: report
data.description:
The U.S. Court of Appeals has reportedly ruled against Novartis (NYSE:NVS) in its bid to prevent the U.S. launch of a generic version of its blockbuster heart failure drug Entresto.

Novartis (NVS) has been seeking an injunction against the launch of the generic, which is to be marketed by MSN Pharmaceuticals, on the grounds it will likely win a patent infringement suit over the product. The appeals court upheld a lower court ruling against Novartis (NVS) handed down in August, according to Reuters.

MSN’s generic version of Entresto, Novartis’s (NVS) best-selling product, was approved by the FDA in July. Novartis (NVS) has been suing MSN to keep the product off the market, arguing it infringes upon its patents for Entresto.

Novartis’s (NVS) patent case against MSN is expected to begin on Monday, Reuters added.

Entresto had sales of $6B in 2023, according to Novartis.

data.title: Google's AI-generated weather forecasts blow away traditional models
data.description:
Google (NASDAQ:GOOG)(NASDAQ:GOOGL) revealed an artificial intelligence-powered weather forecast model called GenCast today that blows away traditional forecasting models in accuracy.

The model produces 15-day forecasts faster and more accurate than other weather prediction systems, including those by the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts. In a study released in Nature, GenCast was more accurate than the ECMWF model 97.2% of the time and 99.8% if lead times were greater than 36 hours.

"An ensemble forecast expresses uncertainty by making multiple predictions that represent different possible scenarios," according to a blog post today by Google DeepMind. "If most predictions show a cyclone hitting the same area, uncertainty is low. But if they predict different locations, uncertainty is higher. GenCast strikes the right balance, avoiding both overstating or understating its confidence in its forecasts."

GenCast also produces forecasts much faster than more traditional weather models. For example, a single Google Cloud TPU v5 can produce a 15-day forecast in eight minutes while simultaneously generating many more. By comparison, traditional physics-based ensemble forecasts would take hours on a supercomputer.

Google said improved forecasting is beneficial for agriculture as well as predicting behavior for extreme weather events, such as cyclones, typhoons and hurricanes.

"We are eager to engage with the wider weather community, including academic researchers, meteorologists, data scientists, renewable energy companies, and organizations focused on food security and disaster response," Google said.
